items.headline: EchoStar's Boost Mobile expands 5G coverage to 12 new markets
items.summary: Boost Mobile switched on its cloud-native 5G network in 12 additional markets, bringing its claimed covered population to roughly 270M and continuing the buildout tied to EchoStar's FCC commitments. The expansion leans on the O-RAN architecture Boost has used since launch, and executives framed it as evidence the network can keep scaling while the parent company works through its spectrum sales — notable because continued buildout spend competes directly with debt service for cash until the AT&T proceeds arrive.

items.headline: 8-K — senior notes offering
items.summary:
EchoStar disclosed a new $500M senior secured notes offering at the DISH DBS level, priced at 9.75% and maturing 2031, with proceeds earmarked to refinance the 7.75% notes maturing in 2027. The filing notes the new notes rank pari passu with existing DBS secured debt and carry a springing maturity if the 2027s aren't fully retired by late 2026.
The item to watch: the indenture adds a covenant permitting asset-sale proceeds (defined to include spectrum sales) to be applied to the new notes ahead of unsecured maturities — the first time spectrum-sale language has appeared in a DBS indenture, which reads as groundwork for routing part of the AT&T payment to this box.
